name: Dimitris Papailiopoulos
bio: Researcher @MSFTResearch, AI Frontiers Lab; Prof @UWMadison (on leave); reasoning in context; learning to remember; agent of agents; babas of Inez Lily.

tweets.text: Holy smokes. What??? “Investigating” is putting it lightly

Btw, a fun footnote: you don’t actually need a computer to train this computer.

Generating training data requires executing one SUBLEQ step which is a fixed function. A combinational circuit can do it, so no CPU needed. 

Then actual training is GD so a bunch of matrix multiplies and weight updates. Also implementable as a fixed-function circuit. Neither of these is Turing-complete.

So: two non-computers, can create a general-purpose computer. 

Turing completeness emerging from non Turing completeness :p

I've seen AI agents discover new stuff. Grounding an llm with a feedback loop is pretty powerful paradigm, and allows you to explore this super cool area. Ton of work happening at all abstraction levels from algorithm discovery, to artifacts that sit at higher abstraction levels. Fun!
